PR surface: Tests +497. Total +497 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds unit coverage rather than reporting a runtime bug. The failure to review is reproducible from CI logs for `check-test-types` and `check-lint` on the PR head.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker is a narrow mechanical test repair in one added file, with CI logs naming the exact type and lint failures.

target.title: test(auto-reply): add unit tests for ACP reset target session key resolution
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `acp-reset-target.ts` module resolves ACP reset targets from sessions and command directives, coordinating service bindings, configured bindings, and raw ACP binding fallback. Despite being a core path in auto-reply reset target resolution, this module had no unit tests to verify the resolution logic.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add unit tests for `resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey` to verify:
- Input normalization edge cases (missing channel/conversationId, empty strings, non-ACP active sessions)
- Service binding resolution (ACP key recognition, non-ACP filtering, whitespace trimming, empty-after-trim)
- Priority chain: service binding takes precedence over active session
- `skipConfiguredFallbackWhenActiveSessionNonAcp` flag behavior (skip vs continue)
- Configured binding resolution with `allowNonAcpBindingSessionKey` flag
- Raw configured ACP session key fallback from bindings list
- `fallbackToActiveAcpWhenUnbound` default/disabled behavior
- accountId resolution path

This improves test coverage and prevents regressions when the function is modified.

## User Impact

ACP reset target resolution now has dedicated unit coverage, reducing the risk of regressions when the function is modified.

## Evidence

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds direct coverage for existing helper behavior rather than reporting a user-visible runtime bug. Source inspection confirms the helper behavior the tests target.

Direct behavior probe:

```text
$ node --import tsx -e "import('./src/auto-reply/reply/acp-reset-target.js').then(({ resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey, testing }) => { testing.setDepsForTest({ getSessionBindingService: () => ({ resolveByConversation: () => ({ bindingId: 'svc-1', targetSessionKey: 'acp:svc-session', targetKind: 'session', conversation: { channel: 'alpha', accountId: 'a1', conversationId: 'c1' }, status: 'active', boundAt: 1 }) }), listAcpBindings: () => [], resolveConfiguredBindingRecord: () => null }); const results = []; results.push({ desc: 'service binding ACP key wins', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg: {}, channel: 'alpha', conversationId: 'c1' }), expected: 'acp:svc-session' }); testing.setDepsForTest({ getSessionBindingService: () => ({ resolveByConversation: () => ({ bindingId: 'svc-2', targetSessionKey: 'non-acp-svc', targetKind: 'session', conversation: { channel: 'alpha', accountId: 'a1', conversationId: 'c1' }, status: 'active', boundAt: 1 }) }), listAcpBindings: () => [], resolveConfiguredBindingRecord: () => null }); results.push({ desc: 'non-ACP service binding filtered out', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg: {}, channel: 'alpha', conversationId: 'c1' }) }); testing.setDepsForTest({ getSessionBindingService: () => ({ resolveByConversation: () => null }), listAcpBindings: () => [], resolveConfiguredBindingRecord: () => null }); results.push({ desc: 'fallback to active ACP session when unbound', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg: {}, channel: 'alpha', conversationId: 'c1', activeSessionKey: 'acp:fallback' }), expected: 'acp:fallback' }); results.push({ desc: 'fallbackToActiveAcpWhenUnbound=false returns undefined', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg: {}, channel: 'alpha', conversationId: 'c1', activeSessionKey: 'acp:fallback', fallbackToActiveAcpWhenUnbound: false }) }); results.push({ desc: 'no channel returns undefined', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg: {} }) }); results.push({ desc: 'no channel but active ACP preserved', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg: {}, activeSessionKey: 'acp:active' }), expected: 'acp:active' }); console.log(JSON.stringify(results, null, 2)); })"
[
  {
    "desc": "service binding ACP key wins",
    "result": "acp:svc-session",
    "expected": "acp:svc-session"
  },
  {
    "desc": "non-ACP service binding filtered out"
  },
  {
    "desc": "fallback to active ACP session when unbound",
    "result": "acp:fallback",
    "expected": "acp:fallback"
  },
  {
    "desc": "fallbackToActiveAcpWhenUnbound=false returns undefined"
  },
  {
    "desc": "no channel returns undefined"
  },
  {
    "desc": "no channel but active ACP preserved",
    "result": "acp:active",
    "expected": "acp:active"
  }
]
```

Targeted test:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/acp-reset-target.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  25 passed (25)
   Start at  23:17:06
   Duration  1.85s (transform 617ms, setup 0ms, import 1.65s, tests 14ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 10.94s
```

Formatting:

```text
$ npx oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/acp-reset-target.ts src/auto-reply/reply/acp-reset-target.test.ts
Checking formatting...

All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 59ms on 2 files using 6 threads.
```

Whitespace:

```text
$ git diff --check
```

## AI-assisted

Prepared with Codex. I reviewed the change, understand the touched code path, and kept the PR focused on the test coverage described above.
